Tests for array and string operations with exception handling in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a short script that tests the behavior of JavaScript built-in objects and methods by executing specific operations within `try-catch` blocks. It attempts to call `Array.prototype.unshift` with an argument, freeze the `Array.prototype` object using `Object.freeze`, and create a new `String` object to call the `split` method. Each operation is wrapped in a `try-catch` block to handle any exceptions that may occur, ensuring that the script does not terminate unexpectedly. This script is likely used to verify the robustness of these operations and to ensure that they do not produce unhandled errors.
